title: On the portfolio roadmap - we need to let the user pick a date range
description: We can use a flux:date-picker using `mode="range"`. Use the boost docs tool to see how to wire it up to livewire.
close_reason: Implemented date range picker for roadmap view. Users can now filter the timeline by selecting a date range. Default is 3 months from today, range persists in URL.

title: On the profile page we seem to have an N+1 issue
description: Repeated select skills.* - repeated many times.  The debug bar suggests the query is being run on app/Models/User.php line 169
close_reason: Fixed N+1 by eager loading user skills and using collection lookup instead of per-skill queries

title: On the roles page - clicking edit doesnt open the edit modal
close_reason: Fixed by adding Flux::modal()->show() call and removing conflicting modal.trigger wrapper from menu.item

title: Export workpackage to html or pdf
description: It would be very useful to be able to export a workpackage/project and all it's subform information to a well presented PDF or html page.  HTML should be our main focus.  We should use the uofg-branding skill to pick the colour palette and tones and also the frontend-design skill to make it look intresting (but not too wild - this is for management to use)
close_reason: Implemented HTML export feature with UofG branding. Exports all project stages with proper field lookups for skills, users, and teams.

title: On the portfolio roadmap - change colour of completed projects to be RGB 27 122 200 instead of dark/black
close_reason: Changed completed project colour to RGB(27,122,200) blue

title: On the staff heatmap - we need to let the user pick a date range
description:
We can use a flux:date-picker using mode="range". Use the boost docs tool to see how to wire it up to livewire.

## Updated Approach (2026-01-12)

Instead of a free-form date range picker, use a simple radio button selector with fixed buckets:
- **10 days** (default - current behavior)
- **10 weeks** 
- **10 months**

This keeps the heatmap grid always at 10 columns, just changing what each column represents. Much simpler than trying to aggregate arbitrary date ranges.

Implementation:
- Add a `viewMode` property (enum or string: 'days', 'weeks', 'months')
- Use `<flux:radio.group>` for the selector
- Modify `upcomingWorkingDays()` in the trait to generate appropriate date buckets
- Aggregate busyness data per bucket (sum/average of projects in that period)

Stakeholder requirement: Need to see heatmap months ahead to plan how new projects would affect the team.
close_reason:
Implemented view mode selector (Days/Weeks/Months) with segmented radio buttons. 

**Implementation note:** Days view uses manually-reported busyness from staff profiles (busyness_week_1/week_2), while Weeks/Months views calculate busyness dynamically from project assignments using Busyness::fromProjectCount(). This was a deliberate choice since manual values only cover ~2 weeks and aren't useful for longer-term planning. Help text was added to clarify this to users. If stakeholder feedback suggests a different approach, we could: (1) make all views use calculated values, (2) show both data sources somehow, or (3) other options.
